page: Amazon Web Services r6idn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions (VNNI), and hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES, VAES, and SHA extensions. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates average performance across multiple categories. It achieved a single-core stress-ng CPU score of 1891.39 and a multi-core score of 15453.56. Memory bandwidth benchmarks recorded 828.26 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 89.42 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, and 54.02 GB/sec for large-block writes.

The instance exhibits strong performance in specific tasks, such as text generation using a small 135M parameter language model, which reached 265.24 tokens/sec. However, larger workloads like text generation with a medium 7B model operate at an average rate of 12.06 tokens/sec. Database operations are supported by the high-speed local NVMe storage, with Redis SET operations reaching 15.8 million operations per second and PassMark database operations scoring 7820.47.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6idn.4xlarge offers a balanced resource density for memory-intensive applications that also require high-speed local storage and network optimization. The inclusion of a 950 GB NVMe SSD and 25 Gbps network baseline reduces the need for separate storage provisioning, though the lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for large-scale deep learning training. This instance is suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers like Redis, data processing, and moderate-scale language model inference.
description: The Amazon Web Services r6idn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory powered by a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD and a 25 Gbps baseline network interface. The processor supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration, delivering average performance in CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks, alongside strong text generation speeds of 265.24 tokens/sec for small language models. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core and high-speed local storage, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dense memory and fast local I/O. It is suited for database operations, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, a 950 GB NVMe SSD, and a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 950 GB NVMe SSD on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, high-speed local NVMe storage, and balanced 25 Gbps network bandwidth.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Dedicated 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processor with AVX-512 support
Bundled 950 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 25 Gbps baseline network speed
Strong performance in small-model text generation at 265.24 tokens/sec
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and data processing
